pattern: Splitting the pattern at its single wildcard reduces the question to whether some window of the text starts with the prefix half and, ending no earlier, finishes with the suffix half.
hints: Split p at the '*' into a fixed prefix and a fixed suffix; the wildcard can absorb any amount of text (including none) between them.
Look for a starting position in s where the prefix matches, then check whether the suffix matches ending anywhere at or after that prefix's end.
Try every start index for the prefix and every end index for the suffix that leaves enough room between them; if any pair lines up, a match exists.

edge_inputs.description: wildcard fills the middle of a match
no arrangement of the wildcard can match
single-character pattern is just the wildcard
pattern longer than the text can never fit
prefix and suffix overlap the same characters and cannot both fit
prefix matches but no valid suffix follows

stress.description: random text and random short pattern with exactly one wildcard
stress.note: small alphabet raises the chance of both matches and near-misses
